summary: End-to-end testing of PortfolioRoaster waitlist MVP. Backend: 9/9 pytest tests PASSED covering root greeting, health, waitlist POST (valid/duplicate/invalid/missing), case-insensitive dedup, count endpoint, and uniqueness/total increment. Frontend: all critical UI flows PASSED on desktop (1920x1080) and mobile (375x812) - landing page loads, hero shows ROAST my portfolio title, Android only + 100% free badges present, marquee visible, 4 canvas (3D scenes) render without console errors or pageerrors (r3f-compat shim effective), waitlist hero form submits and shows YOU'RE IN state with position, duplicate email shows ALREADY IN, FAQ accordion expands/collapses via aria-expanded, footer Features/FAQ anchor links present with correct hrefs and scroll works, sample-roast-card + footer waitlist form present, no horizontal scroll on 375px mobile.

minor.issue: logger is referenced in the except block (line 83 of server.py) before it is defined (logger defined after app.include_router at line 114). If DB insert ever fails, this will raise NameError shadowing the real error. Cosmetic - triggers only on insert failure.

action_items: (Minor) Move `logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)` above the `/api/waitlist` route, or use `logging.getLogger(__name__).exception(...)` inline, so the error path in join_waitlist doesn't raise NameError on DB failures.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: `logger` is used inside the POST /api/waitlist exception handler but is defined further down in the module - works at request time due to module-level resolution, but risky and easy to break. Move logger definition to top of file (right after imports).
server.py uses deprecated FastAPI `@app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown')`. Works today but should migrate to lifespan context manager for future FastAPI versions.

Landing.jsx is ~670 lines containing Marquee/Hero/Verdict/Features/HowItWorks/SampleRoast/FAQ/FinalCTA/Footer. Exceeds the 700-line soft cap if it grows; consider splitting each section into /components/landing/*.jsx for maintainability.
Waitlist position for duplicates is computed via count_documents({created_at: {$lte: existing.created_at}}) - correct but O(N) per duplicate hit; fine for waitlist scale, just noting.
test_credentials: N/A - no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_<uuid>@example.com entries via POST /api/waitlist. Each run adds ~5 rows. No cleanup implemented (no DELETE endpoint); counts remain monotonically increasing which the tests are resilient to (they read `before` snapshot).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest file at /app/backend/tests/test_waitlist_api.py is the regression suite - run with `pytest backend/tests/ -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml`. REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL is loaded from /app/frontend/.env inside the tests. Waitlist has no delete endpoint; tests use fresh UUID emails so they are safe to re-run. r3f-compat shim at src/lib/r3f-compat.js MUST remain the first import in src/index.js - verified working (no x-line-number pageerrors observed during 3D canvas render).
rca of the issue: No active issues. Only finding is the forward-reference of `logger` in server.py's exception block which is a latent bug (will fire only if MongoDB insert fails after module import completes, so it currently works but is fragile).
